observations.recommendation:
1.14 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. recompute the depreciation expenses of infrastructure assets in the
current year without providing a residual value, including the adjustment
to the equity account for the understatement in prior years;
b. prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the appropriate carrying
value of the affected assets noted in this observation; and
c. maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for each road
network to ensure proper accounting of the Road Networks account,
upon reconciliation of the accounting records with the Report on
Physical Count of Local Road Networks submitted by the Municipal
Engineer.

1.15 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Order mandating the -
a) Municipal Engineer to:
i. Conduct an inspection and inventory of the road networks within
the jurisdiction of the municipality, with the assistance of the
Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer (GSO), making
sure to segregate each road asset component, including obtaining
information as to its location and actual condition.
ii. Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network
System, adding therein the assessed values provided by the
Municipal Assessor, and copy furnish the COA, the Municipal
Accountant and the GSO for purposes of reconciliation of records.
b) Municipal Assessor to:
i. Assist the Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer during
the conduct of inspection/inventory of the road lot components of
all road networks within the jurisdiction of the Municipality.ii. Assess the cost of the road network/lot components, assigning such
values to the individual items identified during the inventory.iii. Provide the Municipal Engineer with the assessed values on the
road network/lot components identified, for the preparation of the
inventory report.

1.16 We further recommended that the Municipal GSO maintain a complete Local
Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components, along with a
Local Road Inventory and Road Map.

2.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate:
a) The Inventory Committee to conduct a semestral periodic physical inventory
count of its supplies and materials, and to prepare and submit RPCI to the
Municipal Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the
first and second semester, respectively, furnishing a copy to the Municipal
Accountant.
b) The Municipal Accountant to perform reconciliation of inventory balances
appearing in the books of accounts against the balance shown in the physical
count, upon his receipt of the RPCI from the Inventory Committee, and to
maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each type of supplies inventory to
facilitate proper monitoring of balances.
c) The GSO to maintain Supplies Stock Cards for each type of supplies to
facilitate overall inventory management for the Municipal government, and
ensure that the prepared SSMI reflect only those items that have actually been
physically issued or distributed to end-users.

3.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development
Officer:
a. Ensure that only priority projects that are implementation-ready within
the current budget year are identified and included in the Annual
Investment Program (AIP), considering the capacity of available
technical personnel and the volume of projects funded by national
government agencies and other municipal sources, to facilitate timely
completion;
b. Specify clear timeframes in the AIP for the implementation of each
project, with due consideration to the time required for procurement
processes and the contract duration of each activity; and
c. Regularly monitor project implementation throughout the year,
determine the reasons for any delays or non-implementation, and
institute appropriate corrective measures to ensure the completion of
projects within the prescribed period.

3.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
3. The Municipal Engineer to fast-track the implementation of priority
projects identified for CY 2023 and CY 2024 to avoid further delays and
ensure timely delivery of services to the constituents;
4. The Municipality’s Bids and Awards Committee initiate procurement
activities in accordance with the timelines specified in the Annual
Investment Program (AIP), to facilitate the completion of projects within
the budget year.

4.9 We recommended that the Office of the Mayor and the MDRRM Office
discontinue the practice of charging ineligible expenditures to the LDRRM
Fund, ensuring that before items are charged against the LDRRM Fund, a
review should have been undertaken to check if these are in accordance with
DRRM applicable guidelines. Henceforth, administrative expenses including
procurement of office equipment and regular repairs and maintenance of
vehicles not directly used for disaster risk reduction and management
(DRRM) activities shall be charged to the MOOE under the General Fund.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking
supporting documents on or before May 30, 2025 to avoid the issuance of a
notice of disallowance on the payments noted in Appendix H of this
observation.

5.8 We also recommended that the HRMO strengthen payroll control and
implement a strict verification process over the submission of required
documents before claims for salaries and wages can be submitted for
processing.

Prepare the Disbursement Vouchers to initiate the return of the unused/unexpended funds and forward the same to the Municipal Treasurer for issuance of checks and remittance to the Bureau of Treasury; and

Prepare the Disbursement Voucher to transfer the amount of ₱5,033,165.59 from Due to NGAs account to the appropriate income account in the General Fund and Trust Liabilities account in the Trust Fund, copy furnished the Budget Officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator:
Include in the Annual Investment Plan, only the programs and projects that are ready for procurement and for implementation to ensure their completion within the budget year;

Realign, for other implementation-ready projects, the funds of projects that still have unresolved issues and provide a budget thereto only when all required documents are ready for immediate implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement during the budget year all the identified projects in the Annual Investment Plan that are to be funded from the 20% Development Fund, otherwise, inform the Municipal Mayor, in writing, of the difficulties encountered in its implementation.

implement fully during the budget year the PPAs listed in the LDRRMFIP under Disaster Prevention and Mitigation as well as Disaster Preparedness in order to maximize the utilization of the funds for its intended purpose; and

identify necessary and doable PPAs, to be funded from the Special Trust Fund, that could strengthen the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters that may possibly affect the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and  the Disbursing Officer submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents mentioned in paragraph 5.9 to validate the eligibility and entitlement of the claimants who received the SRA and AHDP totaling ₱815,863.69.
observations.reason: The lacking documents were not submitted to the audit team

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, mandate her staff in-charged with the distribution of goods for COVID 19 response activities, to submit to the Audit Team, the validated list of families affected by COVID 19 per Barangay who received the  goods, attested by the MSWD  Officer and the Municipal Health Officer which shall contain vital information such as the full name of the Head of Family, contents and quantity of the food packs received, to account for the goods distributed.

Lastly, we recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of the inventory items every end of the year and submit the RCPI to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the balances in the Supplies Ledger Card.
observations.reason:
The required documents were not submitted to the audit team







Not Implemented | No physical count of inventory items was conducted at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: Draft the Request of Authority to Write-Off Unliquidated Cash Advances supported with the Ageing Schedule for signature of the Local Chief Executive and submit the same to COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) as soon as it is signed; and
observations.reason: No request of Authority to Write off of the unliquidated cash advances were submitted to the audit team

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Municipal Engineer to: 

conduct immediately an inspection of all buildings and infrastructure assets, including road networks, owned by the Municipality and assess its present value and remaining useful life;

prepare a report thereon with complete description of the buildings and infrastructure assets and submit the report to the Municipal Accountant before the end of CY 2021.

We further 
recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute and record the monthly depreciation of each depreciable asset under the account Property, Plant and Equipment in order to reflect in the books the correct carrying amount of PPEs. Use the report of the Municipal Engineer in computing the depreciation of buildings and infrastructure assets.
observations.reason:
There was a report submitted by the Municipal Engineer for the Infrastructure assets only and the basis for the computation of depreciation as of Dec. 31, 2021 per JEV# GJ100-2021-12-264. Other PPEs are subject to physical inventory by the GSO or the Municipal Treasurer as Chairman of the Inventory.

The office has no sufficient personnel that will focus to compute and record the monthly depreciation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials as an asset under the appropriate Inventory account upon purchase, and to record the related expenses only when supported with the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued from the end-users and Supply Officer with attached pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips.

We also recommended that the staff at the Office of the Municipal Mayor and other offices in-charge with the distribution, submit to the Audit Team the distribution list, duly signed by the recipients with the full name, barangay and quantity of items received, to confirm actual receipt of the goods by the intended families and to account for the procured relief goods.
observations.reason:
Some of the procured food items in CY 2022 were recorded as inventory and some were recorded as an outright expense.

The required documents were not submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Adhere to the composition of the GAD Focal Point System (GPFS) and the creation of the GPFS Technical Working Group to institutionalize gender 
mainstreaming;

Create a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team to take responsibility of assessing the status of PPAs in relation to the plan and existing GAD policy.
observations.reason:
No  executive order on the creation of the GPFS and GPFS Technical Working Group have not been submitted to COA.

The LGU have not submitted until as of report date the executive order creating the LGU GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team, as well as copy of reports/accom-plishments of the team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, with the assistance of the Municipal Agriculturist, formulate and adopt policies and procedures as well as the maintenance plan for the farm tractor as a guide in the utilization of the donated Farm tractor to fully benefit the intended farmer beneficiaries of the Municipality to help increase their production.
observations.reason: The LGU still has no written policy on the use of the farm tractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide a garage for the protection of the farm tractor and accessories against fast deterioration of the equipment.
observations.reason:
A garage has not been provided or constructed
 yet by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive of the Municipality of Babatngon, Leyte instruct the concern Officials of the Local Government Unit to pursue titling of the Land recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: No relevant measure/(s) have been adopted by  Management to comply with the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: Require the submission of the required documentation to warrant the awarding through Negotiated Procurement.
observations.reason: Per record at the Office, the lacking documents have not been submitted yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Take appropriate action on the stale checks and discuss the same with the Municipal Accountant as to its proper disposition; and
Prepare monthly a report of stale and cancelled checks, and the corresponding replacement checks, if any, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry on the cancelled and stale checks based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer in order to restore the amount of cash previously recorded as disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the discrepancies between the accounting records and the records maintained by the Budget Officer and make the necessary corrections, where necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council, update the Annual Investment Plan during the budget preparation stage, and include only in the AIP the priority projects which are procurement-ready that can be implemented during the current budget year.
We further recommended that the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee, and its members, start the bidding process on the procurement of projects in accordance with the timetable indicated in the AIP to ensure completion of the projects before the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairperson of the Municipal DRRM Council, and the Municipal DRRM Officer:
Evaluate and re-assess the need to implement in CY 2024 the remaining 25 unimplemented PPAs listed in the CY 2023 Municipal DRRM Plan (MDRRMFIP) and those to be funded from the Special Trust Fund and exclude in succeeding MDRRMFIP if found unnecessary, and implement during the budget year all identified PPAs listed in the LDRRMFIP; and
Identify and prioritize necessary and doable PPAs, to be funded from the 70% Mitigation Fund and Special Trust Fund, that could enhance the capacity of the LGU to respond to disaster and calamity that may possibly arise.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
gather accounting and treasury records that will prove that the balances of fund transfers had been dormant for ten (10) years or more;
proceed with the process of filing a request for write-off in accordance with the specific guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 as amended by COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and
in the event that the required documents for the write-off cannot be completed or obtained such that the dormant amounts will not qualify for write-off, prepare the Disbursement Voucher for the return to the Bureau of Treasury of the dormant balances in the Due to NGAs account amounting to ₱932,912.16 to extinguish the liability of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the focal person of the Municipal Peace and Order Council, obtain  the  PNP  Certification  containing the concrete details of the peace and order situation in the Municipality of Babatngon, PRIOR to allocating  Confidential  Funds from the budget for Peace and Order Programs to justify its urgency; and include the said PNP Certification as an integral part of the Municipal Peace and Order Public Safety Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the RPTAR with the amount due and collectible for the year, and submit the same to the Accountant at the start of every year.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate record the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record a payable only when supported with complete documents to prove the existence of valid claims and revert those which have no claimants or supporting documents.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Assessor:

initiate and fast-track the updating of the Municipality’s assessment roll through frequent physical survey and cleansing of records; and

prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer, on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.

The Municipal Treasurer:

maintain the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) based on the report of the Municipal Assessor;

prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the RPTAR with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit the same to the Accountant at the start of every year; and

conduct intensive tax campaign and information drive, in addition to the posting of notices, to encourage more property owners to pay taxes. 

We further 
recommended that the Municipal Mayor impose the legal remedies and exercise the taxing authority of the LGU as mandated in the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Assessor initiated the conduct of physical survey

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Human Resource Management Officer:

Develop written policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs taking into consideration the existing regular employees and the actual manpower needs of the LGU;

Require Job Orders to submit primary personal identification, undergo interview and evaluate the performance of each prior to renewal of contracts to give assurance that the risk identified above are addressed; and

Follow the mandated daily 
minimum wage rates prescribed by the RTWPB for Region VIII.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor exert sustained efforts to fill-up the vacant plantilla positions based on firm scrutiny of merit and fitness selection criteria developed by the CSC to ensure high quality government service, subject to PS limitation.
observations.reason: LGU failed to comply the mandated daily minimum wage due to due insufficient budget.

observations.recommendation: Require officials and employees concerned be required to immediately settle/refund in full the unliquidated cash advances or enforce the withholding of their salaries or any money due them if necessary, pursuant to Section 4.1.3, 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Human Resource Management Officer be directed to fill-up the vacant departmental positions, particularly the Municipal Accountant to improve the services/outputs of the Accounting Office. Consider the creation of necessary positions or the abolishment of non-necessary items in the LGU’s overall organizational set-up in order to maximize agency resources for the attainment of the municipality’s functions, targets and programs.

observations.reason: There was a report submitted by the Municipal Engineer for the Infrastructure assets only and the basis for the computation of depreciation as of Dec. 31, 2021 per JEV# GJ100-2021-12-264 but for the Other PPEs are subject to physical inventory by the GSO or the committee . The office has technically/undermanned to compute and record the monthly depreciation rather to compute and record at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation:
4. Prepare the accounting entry to revert to Retained Earnings all Accounts Payables that remained outstanding from CY 2001 to CY 2018 and
5. Record in the Accounts Payable only when the transaction is duly supported with perfected contracts/Purchase Orders, with actual deliveries or services rendered and with duly signed Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documents.

6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop transferring the balances of inter-agency payables to accounts payable at the end of the year and debit the payables only when the amount due has been fully remitted or paid.

7. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Municipal Accountant the stale checks together with the related Disbursement Vouchers for cancellation in the books of the LGU.
8. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to cancel the stale checks and restore the amount to the Cash in Bank-LCCA account and set-up the corresponding liability accounts after it has been identified and documented.

9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials as an asset upon purchase, and to record the related expenses only when supported with the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued from the end-users and Supply Officer with attached pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips.
10. We also recommended that the staff at the Office of the Municipal Mayor and other offices in-charge with the distribution, submit to the Audit Team the distribution list, duly signed by the recipients with the full name, barangay and quantity of items received, to confirm actual receipt of the goods by the intended families and to account for the procured relief goods.
observations.reason: Similar observation is reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
11. Review and update the LDRRMF Investment Plan during the budget preparation in order to provide sufficient funds for each projects, programs and activities (PPAs), instead of including numerous COVID-19 PPAs;
12. Include always in the LDRRMFIP, under a separate caption, the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and
13. Implement during the year the PPAs in the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the budgeted appropriation.
14. We also recommended that the MDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant stop using the LDRRM Fund for PPAs which are not listed in the approved LDRRMFIP to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.reason: Similar observation is reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: 22. Require a close coordination among offices involved in the implementation of projects under the 20% DF in order to achieve completion indicated in the AIP. Make sure the timeline set for every project is strictly followed.
observations.reason: Similar observation is reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommended management formulate and adopt policies and procedures as well as the maintenance plan for the farm tractor as a guide in the utilization of the donated Farm tractor to fully benefit the intended farmer beneficiaries of the Municipality to help increase their production.
observations.reason: Similar observation is reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting
entries to reclassify the various inventory items in the PPE accounts and
the accumulated depreciation attributed to said items.

1.14 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Office Order
creating the Inventory Committee who will implement the guidelines and
procedures in inventory taking, recognition of those found at the station,
and disposition for non-existing and/ missing PPE items for one-time
cleansing of PPE prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
specifically to:
a) prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing the specific
assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date, and a
schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking
activities from start, up to the targeted completion of physical
inventory;
b) submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10)
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking
activities;
c) conduct the physical inventory taking activities in accordance with
the PIP, ensuring all necessary information such as the cost and
specific identifiers like the dates and property numbers of the
properties are indicated, using the prescribed forms of all properties
of the Municipality over a three-month period, starting on July 1,
2022;
d) prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count using
the uniform property identification system for PPE and showing
complete data and clustered by PPE type; and
e) submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team on or before December 29,
2022.

2.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify from Due to NGAs
account the amount of ₱1,586,969.00 to Trust Liabilities account in
the Trust Fund;
b) inform the Municipal Health Officer of the funds available for the
improvement of the facilities of the LGU’s birthing center; and
c) prepare the Disbursement Voucher for the transfer of the total amount of
P305,520.22 to the General Fund bank account to reclassify the funds from
Due to NGAs account to Government Equity and inform the Municipal
Budget Officer of the funds available for appropriation.

3.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the
Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Planning
Officer, identify projects which can be immediately implemented during
the budget year and incorporate the same in the Annual Investment Plan
ranked by priority and monitor its implementation.

3.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer request for
additional technical personnel to assist him in the implementation of the
projects in the AIP and complete the implementation of projects so as to
provide socio-economic development and environmental progress in the
LGU.

4.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Heads of
Offices, who were designated incharge with the implementation of the
programs funded by the Department of Agriculture (DA), DENR, DOH
and DILG, to submit a certification to the Municipal Accountant that
there is no on-going programs funded by said offices.

4.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the DV,
and the Municipal Treasurer issue the checks in favor of the Bureau of
Treasury for the return of the unused funds from NGAs totaling
₱9,501,619.16.

4.13 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer initiate the
closing of the bank accounts with dormant balances and issue the check
in favor of the Bureau of Treasury for the return of the unused funds
totaling ₱271,441.63.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Prepare the Demand Letters, for signature of the Municipal Mayor,
addressed to the Punong Barangays who received the financial
assistance requiring their submission of the Report of Checks
Issued (RCI) and the Report of Disbursements (RD) on the
utilization of the funds they received from the LGU;
b) Monitor the submission of the required reports from the recipient
barangays and record the related expenses upon receipt thereof; and
c) Process the release of additional fund transfers for finansial
assistance only when the required reports on the previous fund
transfers had been submitted.

6.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Disbursing Officer
submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents mentioned in paragraph 5.9 to
validate the eligibility and entitlement of the claimants who received the SRA
and AHDP totaling ₱815,863.69.

6.12 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the
books of the LGU the Utilization Reports on cash advances only if these are
supported with pertinent necessary documents.

7.14 We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Officer:
a) Implement during the budget year the projects, programs and
activities identified in the LDRRM Investment Plan, and give the
highest priority, in terms of urgency and/or impact, on the four
thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management to
enhance preparedness and response capabilities of the
Muncipality’s constituents; and
b) Include in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporate
in the Municipal Development Plan, all programs, projects and
activities (PPAs) to be charged to prior years unexpended DRRMF
in the Special Trust Fund.

7.15 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize, and the
Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant process payments,
for the utilization of the prior years DRRMF in the Special Trust Fund
only when the PPAs are included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and
integrated in the AIP to ensure that the transactions are duly authorized
and to avoid disallowance in post-audit.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
Disbursement Voucher for the transfer of P5,234,289.95, from the Trust
Fund to the General Fund, representing the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF in CY 2014-2016 which remained unutilized after 5 years, and
to record the same as unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer issue the corresponding
check from the Trust Fund, deposit the same to the General Fund bank
account of the Municipality and issue a certification on the availability of
additional source of income for subsequent re-appropriation by the Local
Sanggunian.

8.8 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor, and the members of
the Municipal Development Council, identify projects, programs or
activities for other social services to be funded from the transferred prior
years’ LDRRMF; and to include the same in the Supplemental Annual
Investment Plan to support the Supplemental Budget for approval by the
Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution.

9.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, mandate her staff in-
charged with the distribution of goods for COVID 19 response activities,
to submit to the Audit Team, the validated list of families affected by
COVID 19 per Barangay who received the goods, attested by the MSWD
Officer and the Municipal Health Officer which shall contain vital
information such as the full name of the Head of Family, contents and
quantity of the food packs received, to account for the goods distributed.

9.15 Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record goods
purchased intended for distribution in the appropriate Inventory account
and reduce the account only when distribution is duly supported with the
required documents submitted monthly by the personnel who has custody
of these items.

9.16 Lastly, we recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a
physical count of the inventory items every end of the year and submit
the RCPI to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the
balances in the Supplies Ledger Card.

10.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant
a) Send Demand Letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated
cash advances indicating therein the specific date of submission of
their Liquidation Report;
b) Withhold the salary all accountable officers who fail to adhere with
the Demand Letters, especially those currently employed at the
LGU;
c) Prepare an ageing schedule of dormant unliquidated cash advances
to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks
column the existence of the applicable conditions;
d) Draft the Request of Authority to Write-Off Unliquidated Cash
Advances supported with the Ageing Schedule for signature of the
Local Chief Executive and submit the same to COA Audit Team
Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) as soon as it is
signed; and
e) Refrain from processing Disbursement Vouchers for additional
cash advance of those with previous unsettled cash advances,
including employees of national government agencies.
18. We recommended that the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant submit monthly to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund without need for demand.

19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee follow strictly the guidelines on procurement prescribed by the GPPB and RA No. 9184.
We also recommended that the Supply Officer accept deliveries only when it is covered with approved contract/purchase order, and has undergone the prescribed procurement process mandated in the 2016 Revise IRR of Republic Act No. 9184.

20. We recommended that the Local Finance Committee, through the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, comply with the reporting requirements and posting of the status and posting requirements on the Bayanihan Grant to Municipalities as required in Item 4.0 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 127 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer be directed to comply with the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System to their respective duties and responsibilities provided in Items V to VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015, respectively.

23. We recommended for the creation and operation of the IAS as required under RA No. 3456 as amended by RA No. 4177, to assist in the achievement of an efficient and effective fiscal administration and performance of LGU’s affairs and functions.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive has designated IAS Officer rather for the creation as mandated due to the excess of PS in the budget.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommended that the MPDO be required to conduct periodic monitoring and periodic evaluation/inspection of the status of implementation of barangay projects monitored/evaluated by the municipality, in compliance with Article VI, Section 476(b) of RA 7160. Likewise, ensure that conditions on implementation and liquidation of financial assistance to Barangays as stipulated in the MOA is strictly enforced.

28. Require officials and employees concerned be required to settle/refund in full the unliquidated cash advances or enforce the withholding of their salaries or any money due them if necessary, pursuant to Item 5.1.3, 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
29. Prepare an aging of unliquidated advances and take appropriate action.

30. We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer be directed to fill-up the vacant departmental positions, particularly the Municipal Accountant, and improve the services/outputs of the Accounting Office. Consider the creation of necessary positions or the abolishment of non-necessary items in the LGU’s overall organizational set-up in order to maximize agency resources for the attainment of the municipality’s functions, targets and programs.
32. We recommended management provide or construct a garage for the protection of the farm tractor and accessories against fast deterioration of the equipment.
observations.reason: A garage has not yet been provided or constructed yet by management.

observations.recommendation: 33. We recommended require the submission of the required documentation to warrant the awarding of this project.

34. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive of the Municipality of Babatngon, Leyte instruct the concern Officials of the Local Government Unit to pursue titling of the Land recorded in the books of accounts.

35. We recommended the Head of the Procuring Entity require the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR in the procurement of goods and to submit a complete documentation on all stages of the procurement process.
36. Require the submission of complete documentation of the procurement process up to payment of accomplishment for said project.
37. Require the submission of the required complete documentation of the procurement process before approving payment thereon.
38. Require the submission of the required documentation to warrant the awarding of those projects through Negotiated Procurement.
39. Require the submission of the required documentation to warrant the awarding of this project through Negotiated Procurement.
observations.reason: Per record at the Office, the required lacking documents have not been submitted yet.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit all disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents for all payments made to LIMAR Construction in line with the concrete channel lining and repair of outlet works, pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

9. We reiterate our previous year’s (CY 2018) audit recommendation that  the
   Municipal   Accountant,   Municipal   Engineer   and   General   Services
   Officer/Municipal Treasurer be directed to comply with the Accounting and
   Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System and  to  their
   respective duties and responsibilities provided in Items V to VI and  VII
   of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015, respectively.

5. We recommended that Management:

        a) Re-appropriate for eligible  development  projects  the  savings
           from the implementation of projects under the 20% DF;
        b) Evaluate whether the five PPAs funded from prior  years’  budget
           are still relevant otherwise revert the fund to  other  programs
           and  projects  which  are  more  needed  and   applicable   upon
           recommendation of the Local Chief Executive and approval by  the
           Sanggunian concerned (Section 322 of RA No. 7160); and


        c) Require a close  coordination  among  offices  involved  in  the
           implementation of projects under the 20% DF in order to  achieve
           completion targets indicated in the AIP. Make sure the  timeline
           set for every project is strictly followed.

4. We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of  RA  No.  9184
   and it’s Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) in all stages of the
   procurement of infrastructure projects, goods, and services to  ensure
   transparency, competitiveness and economy in the procurement process.

7. We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction  Management  Office
   (LDRRMO) be required to ensure the optimal use  of  the  LDRRM  Funds  by
   implementing the planned programs and activities under the four  thematic
   areas for Mitigation Fund, which are necessary for the attainment of  the
   goals and objectives of the LGU’s LDRRM Plan.

4.  We  recommended  that  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and   Municipal
   Treasurer be required to remit in  full  the  taxes  withheld  on  income
   payments to BIR, as well as the employees’ and  employer’s  contributions
   and loan amortizations to GSIS  and  Pag-IBIG  Fund,  within  the  period
   prescribed in Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012,  RA  No.  8291  or
   GSIS Act of 1997, and RA No. 9679 or HDMF Law of 2009.

9. We recommended for the creation and operation  of  the  IAS  as  required
   under RA No. 3456 as amended by RA No. 4177, to assist in the achievement
   of an efficient and effective fiscal administration  and  performance  of
   LGU’s affairs and functions.

7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to  prepare  the
   financial statements of the Barangays within sixty (60)  days  after  the
   close of the year as required in Section 10.4 of the Manual on  Financial
   Management of Barangays.

1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer
and General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer be directed to comply with
the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management
System and to their respective duties and responsibilities provided in Items V
to VI and VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015,
respectively.

2.12 We recommended that Management:
a) Prioritize the filling-up of vacant technical positions in the Municipal
Engineer’s Office and the Municipal Planning and Development
Office, and if still necessary, employ technical personnel on a project
basis through contract of service;
b) Require the MEO and MPDO to prepare first the project plans and
program of works of projects before the approval of the Annual
Investment Plan and the Annual Budget. The project plan and program
of work should be the basis in setting definite and realistic timelines of
PPAs in the AIP; and
c) Require the MEO and MPDO to strengthen monitoring of
development projects to ensure these projects are started and
completed on time and, in case of delay, provide the LCE
recommendations to take immediate and appropriate actions.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit
the Memorandum of Agreement per program/projects to COA immediately
after signing of the documents and the release of funds to the LGU. The
Municipal Accountant should also submit monthly the Fund Utilization Report
per program/project to COA and the Source Agency.

4.8 We recommended that the MPDO be required to conduct close
monitoring and periodic evaluation/inspection of the status of implementation of
barangay projects funded by the municipality, in compliance with Article VI,
Section 476(b) of RA 7160. Likewise, ensure that conditions on the
implementation and liquidation of financial assistance to Barangays as stipulated
in the MOA is strictly enforced.

5.7 We recommended that Management:
a. Adhere to the composition of the GAD Focal Point System (GPFS)
and the creation of the GPFS Technical Working Group to
institutionalize gender mainstreaming;
b. Require the GAD Focal Person to gather gender statistics and sex
disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, programming and
policy formulation for the setting up and maintenance of GAD
Database;
c. Prioritize measures on the development and passage of a GAD Code;
and
d. Create a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team to take responsibility
of assessing the status of PPAs in relation to the plan and existing
GAD policy.

6.9 We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 10
of R.A 9184, Section 54(1) and the other relevant provisions of the 2016 Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulation. Management should plan and schedule
procurement based on a quarterly requirement and adopt public bidding to
ensure the LGU could have the opportunity to choose from among bidders, who
could deliver the best and quality items or services at lowest possible cost. The
alternative mode of procurement must be resorted to only in highly exceptional
cases and whenever justified by conditions provided for in the IRR of RA 9184.

7.11 We recommended that the Management:
a) Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to prepare
and submit immediately to COA a Consolidated List of All On-going
GPPAs and those that are to be Implemented in CY 2018, as well as a
monthly Status Report of its Implementation pursuant to Item 2.1 and
3.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
b) Require the department heads to submit to the MPDO monthly report
on the status of implementation of GPPAs of their respective offices to
facilitate timely consolidation of report. The Municipal Planning and
Development Officer as mandated under Section 476 (b.4) of RA 7160
should be required to oversee the submission of reports of each offices
and must see to it that reports are verified, before they prepare a
consolidated report.

8.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare
the financial statements of the Barangays within sixty (60) days after the close of
the year as required in Section 10.4 of the Manual on Financial Management of
Barangays. The Municipal Accountant should submit immediately the Financial
Statements of the Barangays after the lapse of the sixty (60) day period.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer be required to submit immediately the unsubmitted disbursement
vouchers for calendar year 2018, with the complete supporting documents.
Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant should submit the monthly reports
together with the all vouchers, payrolls, official receipts and their supporting
documents in accordance with the provisions of Section 347 of Republic Act No.
7160 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

10.7 We recommended that management submit to the Auditor a status report
on the actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations embodied in the
CY 2017 Annual Audit Report (AAR) using the prescribed form under COA
Memorandum No. 2014-002 dated March 18, 2014. They shall likewise furnish a
copy of the said report to DBM, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the
President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on
Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or
by way of electronic document, as mandated by Section 93 of RA 10924.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be
required to properly withheld income taxes on bonuses and other benefits and
remit to the Bureau of Internal Revenue, pursuant to Revenue Memorandum
Order 23-2014 dated June 20, 2014 and Revenue Regulation 8-2018 dated
January 25, 2018. Likewise, require concerned officials to adhere with the
mandates of the NIRC and other BIR revenue rules and regulations on
withholding of taxes on payment of compensation to employees and the purchase
of goods and services, as well as filing of proper and accurate Returns to the
BIR.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to
prepare and
maintain a Property/Equipment Card for each class of PPE. This P/E Card should
be reconciled with the PPE Ledger Card of the Accounting Office. The Municipal
Accountant should prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the
depreciation for each depreciable assets based on the complete information/records
of properties as provided in the P/E Card and PPE Ledger Card.

We recommend that the officials and employees concerned be
required to
immediately settle/refund in full the unliquidated cash advances or enforce the
withholding of their salaries or any money due them if necessary, pursuant to
Section 4.1.3, 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002. In no case, shall additional
travel advances be granted unless the previous cash advance is first settled as
required in Section 4.1.2 of the same circular.

We recommend that Human Resource Management Officer be directed to
fill-up the vacant departmental positions, particularly the Municipal Accountant to
improve the services/outputs of the Accounting Office. Consider the creation of
necessary positions or the abolishment of non-necessary items in the LGU’s overall
organizational set-up in order to maximize agency resources for the attainment of
the municipality’s functions, targets and programs.

We recommend that the HRMO be directed to specify in the Job Order
Contract or Contract of Service the specific nature of work or duties and
responsibilities and the desired work outputs of the JO personnel, and to submit
copies of these contracts to COA within five (5) days from its issuance as required in
COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer be required to inform COA
within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start
of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or
public notices are already posted at the project site. Likewise, enjoin concerned
officials to comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to
uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of paying regular
expenses on reimbursement basis, instead payment should be made directly to the
supplier or creditor through check as required in Section 2 of COA Circular No. 97-
002. Henceforth, adequately plan its procurement and follow strictly the
procedures laid down in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA
9184.

We recommend that the concerned officials be required to submit to COA
copies of purchase orders and its supporting documents within five (5) working days
from its issuance or perfection in compliance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No.
2009-001. Also, require concerned officials to provide a notice or information on the
delivery of goods within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance, as required in
Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommend that the Social Welfare and Development Officer be
required to formulate a policy guidelines on the grant of financial assistance to
individuals and submit this policy to the Sanggunian Bayan for approval. The grant
of financial assistance shall be based on the approved policy and shall be released
only to qualified recipient with complete supporting documents.

We recommend that the MPDO be required to conduct close monitoring and
periodic evaluation/inspection on the status of implementation of barangay projects
that the municipality has funded, in compliance with Article VI, Section 476(b) of
RA 7160. Likewise, ensure that conditions on the implementation and liquidation of
financial assistance to Barangays as stipulated in the MOA is strictly enforced.

We recommend that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized in
implementing development projects indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.
Likewise, the Local Development Council should prioritize and incorporate in the
Annual Investment Plan of the LGU those projects that are aligned with Section 3 of
the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22,
2017.

We recommend that the officer in charge of payroll preparation be advised
to strictly observe the limitations on payroll deductions so that the monthly net take
home pay of employees will not be reduced to an amount lower than P4,000.00
pursuant to Section 47 of the General Provisions of RA 10924.

We recommend that management monitor the implementation of the
LDRRM Plan to ensure that funds allocated are optimally utilized in accordance
with the timeline of projects. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant and
Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF totaling P
2,146,629.29 to a Special Trust Fund account. Moreover, require the department
heads to submit the required reports under COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-
002.
We recommend that management strictly follow Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the handling of Cash Advances.
observations.reason: Per verification of unliquidated cash advance by the Municipal Treasurer, it was noted that he was granted special cash for 115th Founding Anniversary activities for 2016 last July 21, 2016 which was only liquidated January 23, 2017 which cash was transferred to other personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to the provisions of DPWH D.O. 72, Series of 2012 in the preparation of project plan and cost estimates and to collect from the contractor the advance payment made by the local government.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with our audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to the provisions of DPWH D.O. 72, Series of 2012 in the preparation of project plan and to collect from the contractor the advance payment made by the local government.
observations.reason: No document was submitted to comply with our audit deficiency noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the officials concerned to strictly adhere to the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with our audit recommendation. This is an audit finding in 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor initiate strategies to collect the long overdue loans granted to People’s Organizations (POs) and require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for loans and repayments per PO.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with the recommendation. This is an audit finding in 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive of the Municipality of Babatngon, Leyte instruct the concerned Officials of the Local Government Unit to pursue titling of the Land account to establish ownership.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the grant of PEI should follow the specific provisions of law. Further, direct all concerned officials and employees to refund amount of PEI received otherwise all those involved who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable for the payment thereof.
observations.reason: No document was submitted for our validation of compliance. This is an audit finding in 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to RA 9184 and its revised IRR in the procurement of goods and to submit a complete documentation on all stages of the procurement.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to validate compliance with the audit deficiency noted. A Notice of Suspension was already issued for this disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity instruct the concerned officials to strictly adhere to RA 9184 and its revised IRR in their procurement of infrastructure projects and require submission of complete documentation of the procurement process up to payment of accomplishment for said project.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity instruct the BAC and other concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to RA 9184 and its revised IRR in their procurement of infrastructure projects. Also, require the submission of complete documentation of the procurement process before approving payment thereon.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with our audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to provisions of RA 9184, its IRR and COA Circular 2012-001 and the Memorandum of Agreement in their implementation of RAY projects. Also require submission of required documentation to warrant awarding those projects through Negotiated Procurement.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the concerned officials to strictly adhere to RA 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001. Also require the submission of required documentation to warrant the awarding through Negotiated Procurement.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR in their procurement of infrastructure projects and require submission of complete documentation on all stages of the procurement process.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to validate compliance of the progress payments and mobilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC and other concerned officials to submit the approved change order, revised plans and specifications and other necessary documents required in Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184 and Section 9.1.1.2 of COA Cir. 2012-001.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to validate the approved change order and other supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct officials concerned to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.reason: No document was submitted for our validation of compliance. This is an audit finding in 2011 and 2010.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to conduct a complete reconciliation of balances per Actual Amounts as
presented in the Statement of Budget and Actual Amounts against the Statement of
Financial Performance as of December 31, 2016.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of
accounts the Public Infrastructures of the LGU based on the target of
implementation.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to record in the books of accounts the donated Farm Tractor from
Department of Agriculture R.O. VIII.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to submit immediately the accounts and financial statements/reports of the Local
Government Unit as of December 31, 2016 and thereafter, to comply with the
submission within the prescribed period pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA
7160 and COA Circular 95-006.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND where warranted during post-
audit of transaction documents which were submitted late.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer schedule his workload to
prioritize the conduct of reconciliation with Accounting Office to thresh out the
differences of his cashbook balance against the Cash-in-Bank subsidiary records
considering the materiality of discrepancy amounting to P11,079,783.03.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer on the daily remittance by the RCCs to the liquidating officer of their
collections.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Treasurer to instruct the Revenue Collecting Officers, Deputized Barangay
Treasurers and other Accountable Officers to prepare a Monthly Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms issued to them and prepare and submit an
Consolidated Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms together with
the Individual Reports to the Audit Team not later than the fifth day of the ensuing
month.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to use the cash tickets in the collection of income generating activity of the
LGU for the Busay Falls Resort.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the relevant provisions of
DPWH DO No. 22, series of 2015 in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the
Contract.

This without prejudice to the issuance of ND where appropriate.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials
especially the Municipal Engineer, BAC Members and the Municipal Accountant to
strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 and other provisions of law.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND where appropriate.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineering Officer (MEO) conduct
immediately an inventory on the balance of work still needed to be done to be used as
basis for the preparation of Plan and POW for the provision of additional funding, if
necessary. The MEO should prioritize the implementation of barangay facilities
exposed to elements to avoid their fast deterioration.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND where appropriate.

We recommend that the OSCA and the MSWDO conduct the necessary
verification and home visitation in determining the final list of the Social Pensioners.
Further, immediately after due notification if the social pensioner did not appear in the
scheduled payout the MSWDO should immediately conduct the home visitation for the
delisting and replacement and inform the Disbursing Officer that she/he can immediately
liquidate the unpaid cash advance.

We recommend that Local Chief Executive of LGU-Babatngon should give
priority on the implementation of its Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the officials concerned
to strictly adhere to the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to COA Circular No.
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND if warranted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
officials/employees to maintain Bill Boards for the various Projects of LGU-
Babatngon to determine the actual status of work accomplishment of the project as
of a given date.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, with the assistance of the Municipal
Agriculturist, formulate and adopt policies and procedures as well as the maintenance plan
for the farm tractor as a guide in the utilization of the donated Farm tractor to fully benefit
the intended farmer beneficiaries of the Municipality to help increase their production.

We recommend that management provide a garage for the protection of the
farm tractor and accessories against fast deterioration of the equipment.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the accounts and financial reports of the Local Government Unit and to comply with the submission within the prescribed period pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160, PD 1445, COA Circular 95-006 and EO 292.
observations.reason: Although the LGU is submitting the accounts, it is not submitted within the reglementary period. Further, the year-end financial FS and reports is not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer on the proper recording of collections in the Cashbook; b.) daily sequential issuance of numbers for official receipts; c.) daily remittances of collections by the RCCs to the liquidating officer and d.) monthly inventory of accountability of accountable form to accounting office.
observations.reason: Per cash count, there was found a blank check on hand already signed by the approved Municipal Treasurer without any supporting document to support it.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive stop the practice of countersigning of checks without supporting documents.
observations.reason: Per inventory of checks, we have noted that a blank check on hand was already signed by the approved Municipal Treasurer without any document to support it.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the disallowance of unnecessary expenditures and require proper supporting documents; otherwise issue notice of disallowance.
observations.reason: For 2016, there was no beauty contest sponsored and financed by the LGU; however, a notice of disallowance will be issued because it was not properly supported with documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials/employees to submit the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those to be implemented during the year and inform the audit team on the posting of signboards and/or public notices on the site of the projects.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to validate compliance on this.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implement the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97 - 002 dated February 10, 1997, COA Circular No. 96 - 004 dated April 19, 1996 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 specifically on stopping the granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees if their previous cash advances are not yet fully liquidated; require liquidation within prescribed periods; require written reminders before expiration; suspend salaries and delete name from payrolls until liquidation; institute appropriate action for those who have no salaries that can be withheld.
observations.reason: As of year-end reported unliquidated balances exist which needs to be liquidated; the audit team will issue a demand letter.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: We have not verified compliance because no documents were submitted for our evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Disbursing Officer immediately settle and liquidate the cash advance for calamity expenses granted for typhoon Yolanda, otherwise, the accountable officer will be held liable for the non-submission of the liquidation report.
observations.reason: Although some liquidation was reported, we have yet to verify submission of the liquidation report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor require the accountable officers to strictly comply with the Implementing Guidelines for the Implementation of the Cash for Work Project, DSWD Administrative Order No. 15, Series of 2008 and paragraph 6, Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445 on the submission of complete documents to support payment of any claim.
observations.reason: Deficiencies noted in the documentation were not completely complied by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require officials concerned to: a. Take appropriate action in the return of the unutilized PDAF; b. Coordinate with DBM on the mechanics of returning the said fund.
observations.reason: The management returned the money.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the official in-charge to submit the implementation documents on the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program and to strictly adhere to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.reason: A Solid Waste Management Plan was presented but no accomplishment was reported; further, the LGU's disbursement was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to submit the 2014 GAD Accomplishment Report and every year thereafter and submit the same to the NCRFW for review and endorsement.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted to comply with our audit finding. This is an audit finding in 2013, 2012 and 2011.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Government should: a. Coordinate with DILG Regional Office No. VIII on the appropriate implementation of the project; b. Explain why the project proposal/simplified feasibility study was only submitted after MOA signing; c. Submit a copy of the approved project proposal/simplified feasibility study and copy of the Seal of Good Housekeeping of the LGU.
observations.reason: The management returned the money.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Government Unit must conduct the physical inventory of property pursuant to existing rules and regulations to establish completion of physical inventory taking and accountabilities of officials and employees.
observations.reason: A committee was already created for the inventory of PPE but no documents were submitted to document the physical inventory taking.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require officials concerned to: a) Utilize the PDAF funds received intended for the Scholarship and Livelihood Programs; b) Provide the Official Website address of the LGU in compliance with the posting requirement of NBC No. 529 dated February 21, 2011 and NBC No. 537 dated February 20, 2012; c) Record special purpose programs/project funds receipt such as PDAF to the Trust Fund books of accounts.
observations.reason: The management returned the money.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should require officials concerned to strictly adhere to the requirements of CSC Resolution No. 06-2554 dated December 20, 2006 and to require validation of job orders and compliance with hiring policies and submission of complete documents to support payment of claims.
observations.reason: No documents were presented to validate compliance with our audit recommendation. This is an audit finding in 2011.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC should strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR in their procurement of infrastructure projects and require that procedures for all procurements be followed for all purchases of supplies and materials.
observations.reason: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require officials concerned to strictly adhere to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Annex D.3 and Appendix 4, Chapter III.A.1.a of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and ensure that all submitted documents are complete.
observations.reason: We could not determine the completeness of the document submission especially the DV for the 15% mobilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require officials concerned to follow strictly Section 4, Annex E of the Revised Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct officials concerned to support all procurement for fuel with complete documentation and ensure drivers accomplish trip tickets and passengers sign trip tickets; monthly reports on fuel consumption and official travels should accompany the trip tickets.
observations.reason: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct officials concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and require that procedures for all procurements be followed for purchases of supplies and materials.
observations.reason: Implemented.
